faqs.question: How do I connect to Dynamics 365?
faqs.answer: Gapflow connects to Dynamics 365 using OAuth 2.0 and Azure AD authentication. You'll need to register an application in Azure AD and provide the necessary credentials in Gapflow.

faqs.question: Can I work with custom entities?
faqs.answer: Yes! Gapflow fully supports custom entities in Dynamics 365. You can trigger workflows based on custom entity changes and perform all CRUD operations.

faqs.question: What about API throttling limits?
faqs.answer: Dynamics 365 enforces service protection limits. Gapflow automatically handles throttling with retry logic and implements best practices to stay within limits.

faqs.question: Can I use both online and on-premises versions?
faqs.answer: Gapflow supports Dynamics 365 Online (cloud). For on-premises deployments, you'll need to ensure proper network connectivity and use the appropriate authentication method.

faqs.question: How do security roles affect integrations?
faqs.answer: The user account used for integration must have appropriate security roles and privileges. We recommend creating a dedicated integration user with specific permissions.

faqs.question: Can I sync data across multiple Dynamics environments?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. You can connect multiple Dynamics 365 environments (production, sandbox, UAT) and create workflows that sync data between them.

limits.title: Service Protection Limits
limits.description: Dynamics 365 enforces service protection API limits to ensure optimal performance. Gapflow automatically handles throttling and retry logic.

limits.title: Security Roles & Privileges
limits.description: Ensure the integration user has appropriate security roles and field-level security permissions for all entities used in workflows.

limits.title: Concurrent Connections
limits.description: Be mindful of concurrent connection limits. For high-volume scenarios, consider implementing queue-based processing.

limits.title: Custom Entity Limitations
limits.description: Some advanced features may have limitations with certain custom entity configurations. Test thoroughly in a sandbox environment.
